title: Judge dismisses lawsuit of Babson College student mistakenly deported to Honduras
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge Richard Stearns dismissed the lawsuit of Babson College student Any Lopez Belloza, deported to Honduras in error.
Lopez Belloza was mistakenly deported in violation of a court order; she remains in Honduras, studying remotely.
Her lawyer claims ICE uses detainee transfer tactics to hinder legal action; Lopez Belloza's case is being appealed.

title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson calls Derrick White a 'top 5 player' analytically in the NBA.
White averages 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, and 5.7 assists per game this season with Boston.
Jayson Tatum returns from injury, allowing White to resume his versatile role in Celtics' lineup.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum scored 20 points in his recovery game, helping the Celtics defeat the Cavaliers 109-98.
Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 23 points, 9 rebounds, and 8 assists, filling the gap left by Tatum's previous absence.
Derrick White praised for high-level play; coach Kenny Atkinson called him a top-five NBA player.

title: Former Patriots WR cautions against signing Alec Pierce to a hefty contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots consider Colts' Alec Pierce as a potential top target for quarterback Drake Maye.
Pierce led the NFL in average yards per catch in 2024 and 2025, making him a high-value free agent.
Former Patriots WR Chris Hogan advises against offering Pierce a high salary solely for deep-route expertise.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown police officer Brittany Dougal, 29, died by suicide following a battle with mental illness, the department confirmed.
Dougal graduated from Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy in December and was recently sworn in as a North Kingstown officer.
A visitation and funeral for Dougal will be held in Danvers, Massachusetts, with donations requested in her memory to two charities.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots may consider under-the-radar players like Vikings' WR Jalen Nailor in free agency for added depth.
Giants' TE Daniel Bellinger, with size and blocking skills, emerges as a potential complementary player for Patriots.
Patriots explore EDGE options including Bengals' Joseph Ossai, who recorded five sacks and 43 pressures last season.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady, a Raiders minority owner, reportedly influenced blocking Maxx Crosby's trade to the Patriots.
The Raiders traded Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for two first-round picks, including the 14th overall in 2026.
The Patriots need an edge rusher and are linked to Jaelan Phillips and Boye Mafe ahead of free agency.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Alec Pierce is expected to earn at least $27 million per year, according to ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.
The Indianapolis Colts are trying to re-sign Pierce before the legal tampering period begins.
Pierce expressed interest in testing free agency, seeking a stable franchise and strong quarterback play.

title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Marguerite Flynn, 30, from Hingham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Boston Public Library.
Flynn has faced challenges with dyslexia, ADHD, and a rare vascular condition but finds strength in libraries.
Her fundraising emphasizes the Boston Public Library's role in providing literacy, education, and technology access.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Amy Comander, 51, from Newton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient Christina, facing advanced breast cancer.
Christina has run 20 marathons and qualified for Boston 12 times; she's now enrolled in a breast cancer clinical trial.
Comander supports the Ellie Fund, providing transportation, meals, and childcare for breast cancer patients during treatment.
